reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `source.charAt(curPos + 1) == '\''` fails to handle escape sequences (like '\0', '\n', etc.) which consist of two characters (backslash and the escape char) before the closing quote. It also incorrectly assumes any single character followed by a quote is valid, potentially missing multi-char escape handling or misinterpreting length. Specifically for '\0', `charAt(curPos)` is '\' and `charAt(curPos+1)` is '0', so `charAt(curPos+1) != '\''`, causing it to go to the else block and report an error instead of parsing the escape sequence correctly.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `source.charAt(curPos + 1) == '\''` fails to handle escape sequences (like `\0`, `\n`, `\\`) which consist of two characters between the quotes. It also incorrectly rejects valid single-character constants if they are at the end of the file or followed by a newline without checking the current character's validity properly in all cases, but primarily it misses multi-char escape sequences like '\0'. For '\0', `source.charAt(curPos)` is `\` and `source.charAt(curPos+1)` is `0`, so `source.charAt(curPos+1) != '\''`. The code falls into the else block, reporting an error.
